Operation 'GetExportRecipient' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. The original summary 'Export Recipients Status' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the operation retrieves status information. The description contains procedural guidance mixed with operational details, making it harder for AI agents to understand the core purpose and response structure. The improved summary is action-oriented and specific. The improved description clarifies: (1) what the operation does (retrieves status and URLs), (2) required inputs (export job ID), (3) what it returns (status, details, URLs), and (4) when to use it, making it more interpretable for AI agents while maintaining domain context.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint can be used to check the status of a recipient export job**. 

To use this call, you will need the `id` from the "Export Recipients" call.

If you would like to download a list, take the `id` that is returned from the "Export Recipients" endpoint and make an API request here to get the `urls`. Once you have the list of URLs, make a `GET` request on each URL to download your CSV file(s).

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your recipients regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.summary_suggestion: Check recipient export job status and retrieve download URLs
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the status and download URLs for a recipient export job. Requires the export job ID returned from the "Export Recipients" endpoint. Returns the export job status, completion details, and a list of URLs to download the exported CSV file(s). Use this operation to monitor export progress and obtain download links for your exported recipient data.
data.reasoning: The original summary 'Export Recipients Status' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the operation retrieves status information. The description contains procedural guidance mixed with operational details, making it harder for AI agents to understand the core purpose and response structure. The improved summary is action-oriented and specific. The improved description clarifies: (1) what the operation does (retrieves status and URLs), (2) required inputs (export job ID), (3) what it returns (status, details, URLs), and (4) when to use it, making it more interpretable for AI agents while maintaining domain context.

message: Operation 'UpdateContactDbList' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is too narrow - it only mentions updating the name but doesn't clarify what inputs are required or what is returned. Added clarity about required parameters, return value, and scope of updates to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: This endpoint allows you to update the name of one of your recipient lists.
data.description_suggestion: Updates the name of a recipient list. Requires the list ID and a request body containing the new list name. Returns the updated list object with all properties including the new name. Only the list name can be modified through this operation.
data.reasoning: Description is too narrow - it only mentions updating the name but doesn't clarify what inputs are required or what is returned. Added clarity about required parameters, return value, and scope of updates to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'DeleteContactDbList' has semantic quality issues: missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. Description lacks important side effect information about what happens to recipients and the asynchronous nature of the operation (202 response). Added clarity about the irreversible nature and that recipient records are preserved to help AI agents understand the full impact of this operation.

data.current_description: This endpoint allows you to delete a specific recipient list with the given ID.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a recipient list and all associated recipient relationships. Requires the list ID. Returns a 202 Accepted response indicating the deletion is queued for processing. This operation is irreversible - all recipients will be removed from the list, though recipient records themselves are not deleted.
data.reasoning: Description lacks important side effect information about what happens to recipients and the asynchronous nature of the operation (202 response). Added clarity about the irreversible nature and that recipient records are preserved to help AI agents understand the full impact of this operation.

message: Operation 'ListRecipientsFromContactDbList' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is incomplete - it doesn't mention pagination support which is indicated by 3 parameters. Missing details about what recipient data is returned. Added pagination context and clarification about return data structure for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Retrieve all recipients on a List
data.current_description: This endpoint allows you to retrieve all recipients on the list with the given ID.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all recipients associated with a specific list. Requires the list ID. Supports pagination through limit and offset parameters. Returns a paginated collection of recipient objects containing email addresses and custom field values. Use pagination parameters to control result set size and navigate through large recipient lists.
data.reasoning: Description is incomplete - it doesn't mention pagination support which is indicated by 3 parameters. Missing details about what recipient data is returned. Added pagination context and clarification about return data structure for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'AddRecipientsToContactDbList' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. Description explains inputs well but lacks clarity about the response and operation constraints. Added explicit mention of return status code and clarification that only existing recipients can be added to improve AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Add Multiple Recipients to a List
data.current_description:
This endpoint allows you to add multiple recipients to a list.

Adds existing recipients to a list, passing in the recipient IDs to add. Recipient IDs (base64-encoded email addresses) should be passed exactly as they are returned from recipient endpoints.
data.description_suggestion: Adds multiple existing recipients to a list by their IDs. Requires the list ID and a request body containing an array of recipient IDs (base64-encoded email addresses). Recipient IDs must be passed exactly as returned from recipient endpoints. Returns a 201 Created response. Only existing recipients can be added; new recipients cannot be created through this operation.
data.reasoning: Description explains inputs well but lacks clarity about the response and operation constraints. Added explicit mention of return status code and clarification that only existing recipients can be added to improve AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'AddRecipientToContactDbList' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is too minimal. Missing details about required parameters (recipient ID format), return status, and the constraint that recipient must already exist. Added these details to help AI agents understand the operation's requirements and limitations.

data.current_summary: Add a Single Recipient to a List
data.current_description: This endpoint allows you to add a single recipient to a list.
data.description_suggestion: Adds a single existing recipient to a list. Requires the list ID and recipient ID (base64-encoded email address). Returns a 201 Created response. The recipient must already exist in the contact database; this operation only establishes the list membership relationship.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal. Missing details about required parameters (recipient ID format), return status, and the constraint that recipient must already exist. Added these details to help AI agents understand the operation's requirements and limitations.

data.current_summary: Delete a Single Recipient from a Single List
data.current_description: This endpoint allows you to delete a single recipient from a list.
data.summary_suggestion: Remove a recipient from a list
data.description_suggestion: Removes a single recipient from a list. Requires the list ID and recipient ID. Returns a 202 Accepted response. This operation only removes the recipient from the specified list; the recipient record itself is not deleted from the contact database and remains available for other lists.
data.reasoning: Summary is awkwardly worded with redundant 'Single' qualifiers. Description lacks important side effect clarification - it doesn't explain that the recipient record is preserved. data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to create a custom field.**

**You can create up to 120 custom fields.**
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new custom field for your contacts. You can create up to 120 custom fields per account. Requires a field name and type in the request body. Returns the created custom field object with its assigned ID. Custom fields can be used to store additional contact information beyond standard fields.
data.reasoning: Description is too brief and lacks critical details. Missing information about required inputs (field name, type), what is returned (field ID), and the purpose of custom fields. data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to retrieve all custom fields.**
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all custom fields defined for your account. Returns an array of custom field objects, each containing the field ID, name, type, and creation date. If no custom fields exist, an empty array is returned. Useful for discovering available fields before creating contacts or segments.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to retrieve a custom field by ID.**

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to delete a custom field by ID.**
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a custom field by its ID. Requires the custom_field_id path parameter. Returns a 202 Accepted response. Deleting a custom field removes it from all contacts and cannot be undone. Ensure the field is no longer needed before deletion, as any data stored in this field will be lost.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to delete multiple recipient lists.**
data.description_suggestion: Deletes multiple recipient lists by their IDs. Requires an array of list IDs in the request body. Returns a 202 Accepted response. message: Operation 'AddRecipient' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary 'Add recipients' is too generic. data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to add a Marketing Campaigns recipient.**

You can add custom field data as a parameter on this endpoint. We have provided an example using some of the default custom fields SendGrid provides.

The rate limit is three requests every 2 seconds. You can upload 1000  contacts per request. So the maximum upload rate is 1500 recipients per second.
data.summary_suggestion: Add one or more recipients to contact database
data.description_suggestion: Adds one or more recipients to your Marketing Campaigns contact database. Accepts an array of recipient objects with email addresses and optional custom field data. Supports up to 1000 contacts per request with a rate limit of 3 requests per 2 seconds (maximum 1500 recipients/second). Returns a 201 response with details of created recipients. Recipients are immediately available for use in lists and segments.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Add recipients' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about array input requirement, return value, and side effects (recipients becoming available for campaigns). Added specifics about rate limits, batch size, and immediate availability for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'UpdateRecipient' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary lacks action clarity. Description missing critical details: requires recipient IDs, supports partial updates, return value, and side effects on lists/segments. Enhanced for AI agents to understand update scope and impact.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to update one or more recipients.**

The body of an API call to this endpoint must include an array of one or more recipient objects.

It is of note that you can add custom field data as parameters on recipient objects. We have provided an example using some of the default custom fields SendGrid provides.
data.summary_suggestion: Update one or more recipient records
data.description_suggestion: Updates one or more existing recipients in your contact database. Requires an array of recipient objects with IDs and fields to update. Supports partial updates - only provided fields are modified. Custom field data can be included in update objects. Returns a 201 response with updated recipient details. Changes are immediately reflected in all lists and segments containing the recipients.
data.reasoning: Summary lacks action clarity. Description missing critical details: requires recipient IDs, supports partial updates, return value, and side effects on lists/segments. Enhanced for AI agents to understand update scope and impact.

message: Operation 'DeleteRecipients' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_side_effects. Summary is generic. Description has grammatical error ('deletes' should be 'delete') and lacks critical side effects information - recipients removed from all lists/segments, permanent deletion, and compliance context. Added clarity for AI agents about irreversibility and scope.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to deletes one or more recipients.**

The body of an API call to this endpoint must include an array of recipient IDs of the recipients you want to delete.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes one or more recipients from your contact database. Requires an array of recipient IDs to delete. Deleted recipients are removed from all lists and segments. Returns a 200 response on success. This action cannot be undone. Use with caution when compliance with data deletion requirements is necessary.
data.reasoning: Summary is generic. Description has grammatical error ('deletes' should be 'delete') and lacks critical side effects information - recipients removed from all lists/segments, permanent deletion, and compliance context. Added clarity for AI agents about irreversibility and scope.

Enhanced to explain pagination, parameters, return values, and practical usage for AI agents.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve all of your Marketing Campaigns recipients.**

Batch deletion of a page makes it possible to receive an empty page of recipients before reaching the end of
the list of recipients. To avoid this issue; iterate over pages until a 404 is retrieved.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all recipients in your contact database. Supports pagination parameters to control page size and offset. Returns recipient objects with their IDs, email addresses, and custom field values. Note: Concurrent batch deletions may result in empty pages before reaching the end of the list; iterate through pages until a 404 response is received to ensure complete retrieval. Useful for auditing contacts or exporting recipient data.
data.reasoning: Summary doesn't indicate pagination capability. Description lacks parameter details (page size, offset), return structure, and clear use cases. The pagination edge case is mentioned but unclear. Enhanced to explain pagination, parameters, return values, and practical usage for AI agents.

message: Operation 'ExportRecipient' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Current description is verbose and procedurally-focused rather than API-focused. Reorganized to clearly specify required inputs (segment_ids/list_ids, file_type), optional parameters (max_file_size, notifications.email), and the asynchronous workflow. This helps AI agents understand the operation's contract and expected behavior.

data.current_description:
**Use this endpoint to export lists or segments of recipients**.

If you would just like to have a link to the exported list sent to your email set the `notifications.email` option to `true` in the `POST` payload.

If you would like to download the list, take the `id` that is returned and use the "Export Recipients Status" endpoint to get the `urls`. Once you have the list of URLs, make a `GET` request to each URL provided to download your CSV file(s).

You specify the segments and or/recipient lists you wish to export by providing the relevant IDs in, respectively, the `segment_ids` and `list_ids` fields in the request body.

The lists will be provided in either JSON or CSV files. To specify which of these you would required, set the request body `file_type` field to `json` or `csv`.

You can also specify a maximum file size (in MB). If the export file is larger than this, it will be split into multiple files.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates an asynchronous export of recipients from specified segments and/or lists. Request body must include `segment_ids` and/or `list_ids` to identify what to export, `file_type` (json or csv), and optional `max_file_size` (in MB) to split large exports. Optional `notifications.email` triggers email delivery of download links. Returns an export job object with an `id` used to retrieve download URLs via the Export Recipients Status endpoint. Exports are processed asynchronously.
data.reasoning: Current description is verbose and procedurally-focused rather than API-focused. Reorganized to clearly specify required inputs (segment_ids/list_ids, file_type), optional parameters (max_file_size, notifications.email), and the asynchronous workflow. This helps AI agents understand the operation's contract and expected behavior.

message: Operation 'ListExportRecipient' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Get All Existing Exports' is vague about what is being retrieved. Description is verbose and procedurally-focused. Improved version uses clearer action verb 'List', concisely describes the response structure, and clarifies the use case for discovering export IDs.

data.current_description:
**Use this endpoint to retrieve details of all current exported jobs**.

It will return an array of objects, each of which records an export job in flight or recently completed. 

Each object's `export_type` field will tell you which kind of export it is and its `status` field will indicate what stage of processing it has reached. Exports which are `ready` will be accompanied by a `urls` field which lists the URLs of the export's downloadable files — there will be more than one if you specified a maximum file size in your initial export request.

Use this endpoint if you have exports in flight but do not know their IDs, which are required for the "Export Recipients Status" endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all recipient export jobs, including those in progress and recently completed. Each export object contains `id`, `export_type`, `status`, and for ready exports, `urls` with downloadable file links. Use this endpoint to discover export job IDs when you don't have them, or to monitor the status of multiple exports. Ready exports may contain multiple URLs if the export was split by file size.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Get All Existing Exports' is vague about what is being retrieved. Description is verbose and procedurally-focused. Improved version uses clearer action verb 'List', concisely describes the response structure, and clarifies the use case for discovering export IDs.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to perform a search on all of your Marketing Campaigns recipients.**

field_name:

* is a variable that is substituted for your actual custom field name from your recipient.
* Text fields must be url-encoded. Date fields are searchable only by unix timestamp (e.g. 2/2/2015 becomes 1422835200)
* If field_name is a 'reserved' date field, such as created_at or updated_at, the system will internally convert
your epoch time to a date range encompassing the entire day. For example, an epoch time of 1422835600 converts to
Mon, 02 Feb 2015 00:06:40 GMT, but internally the system will search from Mon, 02 Feb 2015 00:00:00 GMT through
Mon, 02 Feb 2015 23:59:59 GMT.
data.description_suggestion: Searches recipients using query parameters to filter by custom field values. Accepts field_name and value parameters. Text fields must be URL-encoded; date fields use unix timestamps (reserved date fields like created_at and updated_at are converted to full-day ranges). Returns a list of matching recipient objects. This is a simpler alternative to segment-based searching without creating a persistent segment.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description is poorly structured with unclear formatting and doesn't clearly state what the operation returns or its relationship to segments. Enhanced version clarifies input requirements, date handling, return value, and use case for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'SearchRecipient' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary is identical to GET operation and doesn't distinguish this POST method's advanced filtering capability. Description is comprehensive but lacks clear statement of what is returned. Enhanced version clarifies this is a POST-based advanced search, emphasizes the conditions-based approach, and explicitly states the return value.

data.current_description:
Search using segment conditions without actually creating a segment.
Body contains a JSON object with `conditions`, a list of conditions as described below, and an optional `list_id`, which is a valid list ID for a list to limit the search on.

Valid operators for create and update depend on the type of the field for which you are searching.

- Dates:
  - `"eq"`, `"ne"`, `"lt"` (before), `"gt"` (after)
    - You may use MM/DD/YYYY for day granularity or an epoch for second granularity.
  - `"empty"`, `"not_empty"`
  - `"is within"`
    - You may use an [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date format or the # of days.
- Text: `"contains"`, `"eq"` (is - matches the full field), `"ne"` (is not - matches any field where the entire field is not the condition value), `"empty"`, `"not_empty"`
- Numbers: `"eq"`, `"lt"`, `"gt"`, `"empty"`, `"not_empty"`
- Email Clicks and Opens: `"eq"` (opened), `"ne"` (not opened)

Field values must all be a string.

Search conditions using `"eq"` or `"ne"` for email clicks and opens should provide a "field" of either `clicks.campaign_identifier` or `opens.campaign_identifier`.

The condition value should be a string containing the id of a completed campaign.

Search conditions list may contain multiple conditions, joined by an `"and"` or `"or"` in the `"and_or"` field.

The first condition in the conditions list must have an empty `"and_or"`, and subsequent conditions must all specify an `"and_or"`.
data.description_suggestion: Searches recipients using segment-style conditions without creating a persistent segment. Request body contains a conditions array with field-specific operators (dates, text, numbers, email engagement) and optional list_id to limit results to a specific list. Conditions support logical AND/OR operators. Returns a list of recipient objects matching all specified conditions. This is useful for one-time searches without segment overhead.
data.reasoning: Summary is identical to GET operation and doesn't distinguish this POST method's advanced filtering capability. Description is comprehensive but lacks clear statement of what is returned. Enhanced version clarifies this is a POST-based advanced search, emphasizes the conditions-based approach, and explicitly states the return value.

Summary lacks specificity about what a segment is. Description is overly technical and poorly organized, focusing on operators rather than the operation's purpose and inputs/outputs. Enhanced version clarifies that this creates a reusable segment, explains required inputs, specifies return value, and notes the side effect of making the segment available for campaigns.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to create a new segment.**


  Valid operators for create and update depend on the type of the field for which you are searching.

**Dates**
- "eq", "ne", "lt" (before), "gt" (after)
    - You may use MM/DD/YYYY for day granularity or an epoch for second granularity.
- "empty", "not_empty"
- "is within"
    - You may use an [ISO 8601 date format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) or the # of days.

**Text**
- "contains"
- "eq" (is/equals - matches the full field)
- "ne" (is not/not equals - matches any field where the entire field is not the condition value)
- "empty"
- "not_empty"

**Numbers**
- "eq" (is/equals)
- "lt" (is less than)
- "gt" (is greater than)
- "empty"
- "not_empty"

**Email Clicks and Opens**
- "eq" (opened)
- "ne" (not opened)

All field values must be a string.


Conditions using "eq" or "ne" for email clicks and opens should provide a "field" of either `clicks.campaign_identifier` or `opens.campaign_identifier`.
The condition value should be a string containing the id of a completed campaign.


The conditions list may contain multiple conditions, joined by an "and" or "or" in the "and_or" field.

The first condition in the conditions list must have an empty "and_or"`, and subsequent conditions must all specify an "and_or".
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new segment based on specified conditions. Request body requires a segment name and conditions array defining recipient filters (supports dates, text, numbers, and email engagement fields with type-specific operators). Conditions can be combined with AND/OR logic. Returns the created segment object with segment ID, name, recipient count, and condition details. The segment becomes available for use in campaigns and recipient management operations.
data.reasoning: Summary lacks specificity about what a segment is. Description is overly technical and poorly organized, focusing on operators rather than the operation's purpose and inputs/outputs. Enhanced version clarifies that this creates a reusable segment, explains required inputs, specifies return value, and notes the side effect of making the segment available for campaigns.
